Oct 12—post-release check: a small number of users still saw a loop due to cached tokens; mitigation added Oct 13 by shortening token TTL for affected cohorts. Oct 15—Support confirmed complaints stopped.

Compliance checklist progression: Nov 5—privacy review requested for the new telemetry fields. Nov 7—privacy team asked us to remove one field that could infer sensitive attributes. Nov 9—engineering removed the field and updated the schema. Nov 12—data retention policy reviewed; we aligned on 30-day retention for raw events. Nov 14—security required an additional access control audit. Nov 18—audit completed with two minor findings. Nov 20—findings remediated and verified. Nov 22—final sign-off received. Nov 25—documentation updated to reflect the changes, but we agreed to keep the wording internal-only until the next public release notes cycle. Dec 1—monitoring added to detect unexpected telemetry volume increases. Dec 4—monitoring caught a spike from a misconfigured client; fixed Dec 5.

Engineering diary excerpt: Dec 12—noticed a gradual memory increase in the feed screen after extended scrolling. Dec 13—instrumentation added; issue traced to image prefetch holding references longer than needed. Dec 14—implemented bounded cache and ensured eviction on background. Dec 15—profiling showed stabilization, but one rare crash appeared when returning from a deep link. Dec 16—added a guard around view model rehydration; crash no longer reproducible. Dec 18—QA signed off for the performance fix, and we scheduled it for the Jan release train instead of a mid-December patch due to holiday freeze. Dec 20—shared a short summary with Product about the impact on perceived smoothness. Dec 22—checked crash metrics; no new spikes. Jan 3—revalidated on the first business day back and updated the rollout checklist.

Cross-team sync timeline: Jan 22—Payments team warned of an upcoming API version deprecation in mid-Feb. Jan 23—we opened a ticket to migrate to the new version and asked for a sandbox key. Jan 26—sandbox key delivered; initial integration failed due to missing header requirements. Jan 27—Payments clarified the headers and provided example responses. Jan 29—migration completed in staging; contract tests passed. Feb 1—QA verified purchase, refund, and restore flows. Feb 3—Product requested that we hold the change until after a marketing campaign launch. Feb 6—we negotiated a compromise: include the migration in the next release but keep it dark-launched until Feb 12. Feb 12—dark launch enabled; no errors observed. Feb 14—full enablement completed, ahead of the deprecation window.

Weekly sync recap (captured same day): Today (Mon, Feb 16) we aligned on the rollout timeline for the notifications revamp. Last Friday we agreed to freeze UI strings; as of this morning, localization is unblocked and the remaining risk is QA capacity. Tomorrow, engineering will cut the RC build by 11am PT, and product will send the stakeholder update by end of day. On Wednesday we plan to run a 30-minute canary with 5% of traffic; if crash-free rate stays above last week’s baseline through Thursday noon, we’ll expand to 25% that afternoon. Note: last month’s incident review still requires a short addendum—legal asked on Feb 12 whether we log device identifiers; privacy confirmed we only store ephemeral tokens. Action items: confirm support staffing for the weekend, and re-check the Monday holiday calendar impact on review turnaround.

Quarter planning notes (time-anchored): In Q4 we deferred the billing export rework due to the vendor contract renegotiation, and we documented that decision during the Nov 3 steering meeting. As of mid-January, procurement cleared the new terms, so the earliest start date is the first sprint after Feb 24. However, the stakeholder expectation set in December assumed a March 1 beta; we need to reset that messaging because dependency work is still in flight. By next Monday, analytics will finalize the schema mapping; two weeks later, we can run the first internal dry-run with finance. If the dry-run passes, we target a limited pilot in early April and a general rollout by the end of that month. Reminder: the last time we attempted this (late summer), we hit rate-limit failures from the external API and had to pause for three days.
